text_chunks.ops: Sort the bars by “Production in million liters” to pick the three highest and the three lowest values.
text_chunks.ops2: Calculate the average production of the top three and the average production of the bottom three.
text_chunks.ops3: Multiply the bottom-three average by two.
text_chunks.ops4: Compare the top-three average to that doubled value.

text_chunks.ops: Treat the Northern Hemisphere as the continents Asia, Africa, Europe, and Northern America shown in the chart. Sum their population shares to get the Northern Hemisphere total.
text_chunks.ops2: Divide Asia’s share by that total and convert to a percentage to get 65.03%.

text_chunks.ops: List the fatalities values for all years and sort them from smallest to largest.
text_chunks.ops2: Locate the three middle values in the sorted list (the median trio for an odd count of years).
text_chunks.ops3: Add those three middle values together.

text_chunks.ops: Exclude the region with the tallest bar from the set of regions.
text_chunks.ops2: Calculate the average revenue of the remaining regions.
text_chunks.ops3: Compare each region’s revenue against that average.
text_chunks.ops4: Count the regions whose bars fall below the computed average.

text_chunks.ops: Sort the bars by Year in ascending order.
text_chunks.ops2: Calculate the year-over-year change by subtracting each year’s shipments from the previous year’s shipments.
text_chunks.ops3: Mark each year where this change is greater than 3 million.

text_chunks.ops: Calculate the absolute difference in SharePercentage between the two AgeGroup bars within each Period.
text_chunks.ops2: Sort the Periods by this difference and select the three largest gaps.

text_chunks.ops: Sort the categories by the Female “Share of employees” from lowest to highest.
text_chunks.ops2: Select the category that ranks second in that ordering.
text_chunks.ops3: Read the Male “Share of employees” value for that category from the corresponding bar.

text_chunks.ops: Focus on the bars colored for 2019 in each Diet Type panel.
text_chunks.ops2: Keep only diet types whose 2019 value is strictly greater than 0.03.

text_chunks.ops: Get Germany’s value for the “Gasoline or diesel (ICE)” segment from the chart.
text_chunks.ops2: Average the “Gasoline or diesel (ICE)” values across all the other countries.
text_chunks.ops3: Compare Germany’s value to that average to determine whether it is higher.

text_chunks.ops: Read the Percentage values for White and Black in that segment from the chart.
text_chunks.ops2: Subtract the White value from the Black value to get the difference.

text_chunks.ops: Compare Thailand and Philippines segment values within each year on the chart.
text_chunks.ops2: Mark each year where Thailand’s Revenue_Million_USD is greater than the Philippines’ value.

text_chunks.ops: Sum the three Mart segments within each Year bar to get total net sales per year.
text_chunks.ops2: Calculate the year-over-year growth rate for each adjacent pair of years by dividing the change in totals by the prior year’s total.
text_chunks.ops3: Compare these growth rates across all years. Select the year associated with the largest growth rate, which is 2021.

text_chunks.ops: Sum the Canada and Other International values across the years 2017 through 2020.
text_chunks.ops2: Compare that combined total to the US segment value for the year 2020.

text_chunks.ops: Filter the chart to the years 2010 through 2019 inclusive.
text_chunks.ops2: List the ten “Number of facilities” values for those years and sort them from smallest to largest.
text_chunks.ops3: Take the two middle values (5th and 6th) and calculate their average to get the median.

text_chunks.ops: Find the minimum and maximum values on the chart across all years.
text_chunks.ops2: Calculate their average by adding them and dividing by two.
text_chunks.ops3: Read the chart value at year 2010.
text_chunks.ops4: Compare the min–max average to the 2010 value to determine whether it is greater.

text_chunks.ops: Find the maximum Percentage among the points where Role is Large.
text_chunks.ops2: Get the minimum Percentage among the points where Role is Small.
text_chunks.ops3: Subtract the small-role minimum from the large-role maximum to obtain the difference.
text_chunks.ops4: Average the Percentage values across all points in the chart.
text_chunks.ops5: Compare the computed difference with that overall average.

text_chunks.ops: Sort the “No” line values by share to find the year with the highest percentage of no.
text_chunks.ops2: Read the “Share of respondents” value for that year.
text_chunks.ops3: Sort the “Yes” line values by share to find the year with the third-highest percentage of yes.
text_chunks.ops4: Subtract the third-highest “Yes” value from the highest “No” value.

text_chunks.ops: Compute the mean Percentage_of_Respondents for Dissatisfied across all years and the mean for Satisfied across all years.
text_chunks.ops2: Subtract the Satisfied mean from the Dissatisfied mean to get the difference between their means.
text_chunks.ops3: Calculate the yearly gap by subtracting Satisfied from Dissatisfied for each year.
text_chunks.ops4: Compare each yearly gap to the mean-difference value.
text_chunks.ops5: Count the years where the yearly gap is smaller.

text_chunks.ops: Locate the year 2005 on the chart. Read the share for the Engaged line and the Actively disengaged line at that year.
text_chunks.ops2: Subtract the disengaged share from the engaged share to get the difference.
